combat_history.event: FS Courbet conducted surveillance and escort missions during NATO intervention in Libya, demonstrating stealth frigate utility in contested airspace
combat_history.significance: First major combat deployment validated stealth design philosophy in modern threat environment

combat_history.event: La Fayette class frigates provided offshore support and logistics security for Mali intervention forces
combat_history.significance: Demonstrated utility for expeditionary operations supporting land campaigns

combat_history.event: FS Aconit conducted anti-ISIS maritime security operations in Eastern Mediterranean
combat_history.significance: Proved value of low-signature platforms for extended patrol missions in hostile areas

combat_history.event: Multiple La Fayette class ships conducted freedom of navigation operations in South China Sea
combat_history.significance: Stealth characteristics valuable for intelligence gathering and presence operations near peer adversaries

modernization.description: La Fayette class will be gradually replaced by additional FREMM frigates and future FDI frigates. Some La Fayette hulls may receive life extension upgrades.
modernization.impact: End of dedicated stealth frigate concept in favor of more heavily armed multi-mission designs

modernization.description: Replacement of original Crotale point defense with Aster 15 VLS system, upgraded radar and combat management systems
modernization.impact: Significantly improved air defense capability while maintaining stealth characteristics

modernization.description: Potential upgrade of Sea Tiger radar to newer AESA systems, electronic warfare suite enhancement
modernization.impact: Would extend service life and improve detection capabilities against stealth threats

vulnerabilities.description: Only 8 VLS cells and 8 anti-ship missiles severely limit sustained combat capability compared to peers
vulnerabilities.context: Against peer navies with large missile salvos, La Fayette class would be quickly overwhelmed
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Designed to avoid detection rather than survive heavy engagement; operated in task groups

vulnerabilities.description: DRBV-15C radar lacks modern AESA capability and has limited performance against low-RCS targets
vulnerabilities.context: Modern stealth missiles and aircraft can potentially negate the frigate's own stealth advantage
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Planned sensor upgrades, but budget constraints may limit implementation

vulnerabilities.description: Single hull-mounted sonar with no towed array limits submarine detection capability
vulnerabilities.context: Critical weakness in increasingly submarine-heavy threat environment
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Heavy reliance on embarked helicopter for ASW operations

vulnerabilities.description: Limited EW suite compared to modern standards, vulnerable to advanced jamming
vulnerabilities.context: Peer adversaries have developed sophisticated EW capabilities that could negate stealth advantages
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Modernization programs include EW upgrades, but implementation uncertain

operational_patterns.typical_deployment: Independent patrol missions, task group escort, sovereignty operations in overseas territories
operational_patterns.typical_task_group: Often operates independently or with 1-2 support vessels, occasionally with carrier battle group
operational_patterns.readiness_notes: High availability rate due to diesel propulsion, but aging systems requiring increased maintenance

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Type 054A has 32 VLS cells vs 8, but much higher RCS. La Fayette prioritizes stealth over firepower while Type 054A is conventional design with heavier armament.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Stealth vs firepower philosophy - which approach wins in modern naval combat?

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Gorshkov has far superior missile armament (32+ VLS) and sensors, but traditional high-visibility design. Represents opposite design philosophy to La Fayette.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Cold War mentality vs modern stealth - how naval design philosophies diverged

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Type 23 emphasizes ASW with towed sonar arrays and has superior Sea Wolf missile system, but conventional RCS. More capable in ASW, less survivable in missile threat environment.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Specialist ASW frigate vs stealth multi-role - different solutions to NATO requirements

peer_comparisons.key_differences: MEKO A-200 offers modular design flexibility and heavier armament options, but lacks integrated stealth features. Competes in same export market with different value propositions.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Modular flexibility vs integrated stealth - competing frigate export philosophies

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Sachsen class has superior APAR radar and 32 VLS cells, designed as area defense frigate vs La Fayette's stealth patrol role. Much more expensive but more capable.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: High-end vs cost-effective European frigate approaches
